owasp_top10: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
mitre_attack: T1070.001 - Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
T1562.002 - Impair Defenses: Disable Windows Event Logging
T1070.003 - Indicator Removal: Clear Command History
cwe: CWE-117 - Improper Output Neutralization for Logs

threat.title: Missing Audit Logs for Critical Actions in Spring Boot and AWS
threat.description: When critical business operations (financial transactions, access control changes, data modifications) lack comprehensive audit logging, attackers can perform malicious actions with impunity. Without forensic evidence, organizations cannot detect breaches, investigate incidents, or prove compliance. This vulnerability is particularly severe in regulated industries where audit trails are legally mandated.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker compromises low-privilege user account through phishing
2. Escalates privileges by modifying role assignments in the database directly
3. Accesses sensitive customer financial records and initiates unauthorized wire transfers
4. Deletes evidence of transactions from application database tables
5. Modifies access control policies to create backdoor admin accounts
6. Organization discovers breach weeks later but cannot determine scope, timeline, or attribution due to missing audit logs

Forensic investigation fails because:
- No record of privilege escalation attempt
- No audit trail of database modifications
- No logs showing which records were accessed
- No evidence of unauthorized transaction initiation
- Cannot prove regulatory compliance during incident timeframe
prerequisites: Application does not log critical business operations
Database audit logging is disabled or not configured
No centralized log aggregation (SIEM) in place
CloudTrail not enabled or not monitoring application APIs
No alert mechanisms for sensitive actions
Log retention policy inadequate or non-existent

cve_references.description: Insufficient logging is a design flaw rather than a specific CVE

real_world_breaches.impact: 106 million customers affected; $80 million fine from OCC; $190 million class action settlement
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient logging and monitoring allowed attacker (former AWS employee) to exfiltrate data over several months undetected. CloudTrail logs were not properly monitored for anomalous API calls.

real_world_breaches.impact: 18,000+ organizations compromised; attackers maintained access for 9 months undetected
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of comprehensive audit logging in Orion platform prevented early detection. Attackers exploited the absence of detailed API activity logs to move laterally and exfiltrate data without triggering alerts.

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Critical financial transactions not logged, enabling fraud without detection

security_research.title: OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet
security_research.key_finding: 66% of breaches take months to discover due to insufficient logging and monitoring

security_research.title: Verizon 2023 Data Breach Investigations Report
security_research.key_finding: 87% of breaches took weeks or longer to discover; median time to discovery is 21 days

security_research.title: AWS Security Best Practices - Logging and Monitoring
security_research.key_finding: Organizations without CloudTrail enabled average 3x longer incident response times

mitigations.title: Implement Comprehensive Audit Logging with Spring AOP
mitigations.description: Use Spring Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP) to automatically capture audit logs for all critical business operations. This creates a centralized, declarative approach to logging that cannot be bypassed by developers and ensures consistent audit trails across the application.
nist_csf: DE.CM-6 - External service provider activity is monitored
PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined and documented
validation_steps: Verify @AuditLog annotations on all sensitive methods
Test that audit events are persisted to database
Confirm sensitive data (passwords, tokens) is redacted
Validate audit log includes user ID, timestamp, IP, action
Test that audit logging failures don't break business operations
Query audit_events table to verify log retention

mitigations.title: Enable AWS CloudTrail for API-Level Audit Logging
mitigations.description: Configure AWS CloudTrail to capture all AWS API calls made by the application, including S3 access, DynamoDB operations, Lambda invocations, and IAM changes. CloudTrail provides immutable, tamper-proof audit logs that satisfy regulatory requirements and enable forensic investigation.

tools.name: AWS CloudTrail
tools.cost: $2.00 per 100,000 management events; $0.10 per 100,000 data events

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch Logs
tools.cost: $0.50 per GB ingested; $0.03 per GB stored per month

tools.name: AWS Athena
tools.type: SQL Query Engine for CloudTrail Logs

nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Verify CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
Confirm log file validation is enabled
Test S3 bucket encryption with KMS
Validate data events are captured for sensitive resources
Query CloudTrail via Athena to verify log completeness
Test CloudWatch alarms trigger on unauthorized access
Verify log retention meets compliance requirements (7 years PCI-DSS)

mitigations.title: Implement Structured Logging with Correlation IDs and SIEM Integration
mitigations.description: Adopt structured JSON logging with correlation IDs to trace requests across distributed systems. Integrate with a SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) platform for centralized log aggregation, analysis, and alerting on security-relevant events.
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Verify all logs include correlation ID in MDC
Test correlation ID propagates across microservices
Confirm structured JSON format with required fields
Validate SIEM ingestion via Logstash
Create Elasticsearch index pattern for security logs
Build Kibana dashboard for security event visualization
Test log search by correlation ID, user ID, event type

indicators: Absence of logs during known administrative sessions
Gaps in audit trail timestamps indicating log deletion
Failed forensic investigations due to missing evidence
Inability to correlate user actions across systems
Compliance audit findings for insufficient logging
CloudTrail disabled or S3 bucket deleted
Unusual privilege escalations with no corresponding logs
Database modifications with no application-level audit trail

log_queries.query:
// Detect absence of critical audit events
event.category: "authentication" OR event.category: "iam"
| stats count by user.id, event.action
| where count < expected_threshold

// Detect gaps in audit log timestamps
event.module: "audit"
| sort @timestamp
| eval time_diff = @timestamp - lag(@timestamp)
| where time_diff > 3600000  // 1 hour gap

// Detect CloudTrail disabled events
event.provider: "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com"
AND event.action: "StopLogging"

// Detect unauthorized privilege changes without audit logs
event.action: "user.role.updated" 
AND NOT event.outcome: "success"
AND user.roles: "admin"

# Detect missing audit logs for financial transactions
index=app_logs sourcetype=financial_transaction
| stats count by user_id, transaction_type, _time
| where count=0 AND transaction_type="wire_transfer"

# Detect CloudTrail log gaps
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| streamstats current=f last(_time) as last_time by eventSource
| eval gap=_time - last_time
| where gap > 3600
| table _time, eventSource, eventName, userIdentity.principalId, gap

# Alert on missing audit logs for sensitive data access
index=app_logs event_type="data.access" resource_type="customer_pii"
| timechart span=1h count
| where count=0

// Detect disabled audit logging
AzureActivity
| where OperationNameValue =~ "Microsoft.Insights/DiagnosticSettings/Write"
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| extend properties = todynamic(Properties)
| where properties.logs[0].enabled == false
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId, OperationNameValue

// Detect administrative actions without corresponding audit events
let AdminActions = AzureActivity
    | where Authorization contains "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write"
    | project TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId;
let AuditLogs = AzureActivity
    | where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
    | project TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId;
AdminActions
| join kind=leftanti AuditLogs on Caller, ResourceId
| summarize MissingAuditCount = count() by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)

# Detect missing CloudTrail logs
fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.principalId, errorCode
| filter eventSource = "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com"
| filter eventName = "StopLogging" or eventName = "DeleteTrail"
| sort @timestamp desc

# Detect IAM changes without audit trail
fields @timestamp, eventName, requestParameters.userName, responseElements.role.roleName
| filter eventSource = "iam.amazonaws.com"
| filter eventName like /Put|Create|Delete|Attach|Detach/
| filter ispresent(errorCode)
| stats count() by eventName, userIdentity.principalId

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch Logs Insights
tools.usage: Query CloudTrail and application logs for anomalies

tools.name: Elasticsearch
tools.usage: Full-text search across structured audit logs

tools.name: AWS Athena
tools.usage: Query CloudTrail logs stored in S3 using SQL
